questions.question: What is the primary use of dimensionality reduction in machine learning?
options: To increase model complexity
To reduce the size of the feature space
To improve model interpretability
To increase model accuracy
questions.explanation: Dimensionality reduction is used to reduce the number of features in a dataset, making it easier to model and analyze.

questions.question: What is the main difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
options: Supervised learning is used for classification tasks, while unsupervised learning is used for regression tasks
Supervised learning is used for regression tasks, while unsupervised learning is used for classification tasks
Supervised learning is used when the target variable is known, while unsupervised learning is used when the target variable is unknown
Supervised learning is used when the target variable is unknown, while unsupervised learning is used when the target variable is known
questions.explanation: Supervised learning is used when the target variable is known, while unsupervised learning is used when the target variable is unknown.

questions.question: What is the purpose of regularization in machine learning?
options: To prevent overfitting by adding a penalty term to the loss function
To improve model interpretability by adding more features
To increase model accuracy by adding more training data
To decrease model complexity by removing features
questions.explanation: Regularization is used to prevent overfitting by adding a penalty term to the loss function.

questions.question: What is the main goal of cross-validation in machine learning?
options: To evaluate the performance of a model on a single dataset
To estimate the performance of a model on unseen data
To compare the performance of different models
To identify the most important features in a dataset
questions.explanation: Cross-validation is used to estimate the performance of a model on unseen data.

questions.question: What is the difference between linear regression and logistic regression?
options: Linear regression is used for classification tasks, while logistic regression is used for regression tasks
Linear regression is used when the target variable is continuous, while logistic regression is used when the target variable is binary
Linear regression is used when the target variable is known, while logistic regression is used when the target variable is unknown
Linear regression is used when the target variable is unknown, while logistic regression is used when the target variable is known
questions.explanation: Linear regression is used when the target variable is continuous, while logistic regression is used when the target variable is binary.
